group_teams.description:
Ordered map of prometheus group regexes to chronosphere team slug. This is used to
determine which team will own the collection created for each prometheus group..

notification_policy_team_slug.description: The team that will own the generated notification policy

ChronoConfigCollectionsConfig.title: Configuration for when ContainerType == COLLECTIONS or DEFAULT is collections

create_if_missing.description: If `true`, the CloudIntegration will be created if it does not already exist, identified by `slug`. If `false`, an error will be returned if the CloudIntegration does not already exist.

dry_run.description: If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating or updating the CloudIntegration. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the CloudIntegration. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

create_if_missing.description: If `true`, the CommandCenterGroup will be created if it does not already exist, identified by `slug`. If `false`, an error will be returned if the CommandCenterGroup does not already exist.

dry_run.description: If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating or updating the CommandCenterGroup. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the CommandCenterGroup. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

create_if_missing.description: If `true`, the Dashboard will be created if it does not already exist, identified by `slug`. If `false`, an error will be returned if the Dashboard does not already exist.
If `true`, the LinkTemplate will be created if it does not already exist, identified by `slug`. If `false`, an error will be returned if the LinkTemplate does not already exist.

dry_run.description: If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating or updating the LinkTemplate. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the LinkTemplate. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

create_if_missing.description: If `true`, the NoopEntity will be created if it does not already exist, identified by `slug`. If `false`, an error will be returned if the NoopEntity does not already exist.
If `true`, the Notebook will be created if it does not already exist, identified by `slug`. If `false`, an error will be returned if the Notebook does not already exist.
If `true`, the ObjectDiscoveryRule will be created if it does not already exist, identified by `slug`. If `false`, an error will be returned if the ObjectDiscoveryRule does not already exist.
If `true`, the SavedTraceSearch will be created if it does not already exist, identified by `slug`. If `false`, an error will be returned if the SavedTraceSearch does not already exist.
If `true`, the Service will be created if it does not already exist, identified by `slug`. If `false`, an error will be returned if the Service does not already exist.

dry_run.description: If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating or updating the Service. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the Service. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

create_if_missing.description: If `true`, the SyntheticGlobalVariable will be created if it does not already exist, identified by `slug`. If `false`, an error will be returned if the SyntheticGlobalVariable does not already exist.

dry_run.description: If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating or updating the SyntheticGlobalVariable. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the SyntheticGlobalVariable. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

create_if_missing.description: If `true`, the SyntheticTest will be created if it does not already exist, identified by `slug`. If `false`, an error will be returned if the SyntheticTest does not already exist.

dry_run.description: If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating or updating the SyntheticTest. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the SyntheticTest. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

create_if_missing.description: If `true`, the TraceJaegerRemoteSamplingStrategy will be created if it does not already exist, identified by `slug`. If `false`, an error will be returned if the TraceJaegerRemoteSamplingStrategy does not already exist.

dry_run.description: If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating or updating the TraceJaegerRemoteSamplingStrategy. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the TraceJaegerRemoteSamplingStrategy. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

GcpConfigMetricGroup.description: MetricGroup defines a set of metrics to collect from a GCP project.

project_id.description: GCP project ID that has access to the metric data.

prefixes.description: List of GCP metric prefixes to ingest (e.g., "compute.googleapis.com/").

GcpConfigServiceAccount.description: ServiceAccount specifies the GCP service account to impersonate for API calls.

client_email.description: Email address of the service account to impersonate.

HttpTestConfigContentType.description:
Content type sent with the request. Mapped to a Content-Type request
header. Validation rejects setting this alongside an explicit
Content-Type header.

HttpTestConfigHeader.description:
Single HTTP request header. Modeled as a message rather than
map<string,string> to preserve order and leave room for future
per-header fields. Duplicate names (case-insensitive) are rejected
by validation.

HttpTestConfigHttpAuth.description:
One of the following auth mechanisms may be set; additional auth types
(OAuth, APITokenAuth) will be added as siblings. Validation enforces
exactly-one when authentication is set.

HttpTestConfigHttpVersion.description:
HTTP protocol version used for the request. When unset, the default is to
negotiate HTTP/2 with fallback to HTTP/1.1.

name.description: name of the field as it will be referred to in queries; could be omitted and targeted_field would be used as the PK name in that case

source.description: source is the name of the source field

operation.description: The operation to which this specific strategy should apply.

min_time.description: Start time of the search interval.

max_time.description: End time of the search interval.

time.description: Time around which the search will performed.

start_relative_offset_secs.description: The duration, in seconds, from now to the beginning of the search interval.

end_relative_offset_secs.description: The duration, in seconds, from now to the end of the search interval.

diff.description: TODO: do we want to keep diff in the public API?

severity_label_name.description:
Customer-specific name of alert rule label used to determine severity.
If omitted, the Chronosphere default of "severity" is used.

assign_monitor_slugs.description: TODO: before configv1 - is this still required? does chronoctl set it?

monitor_signal_per_series.description: If true, each monitor will not group alert notifications.

infer_monitor_signals.description: Controls whether we try to infer monitor signals from the alertmanager route tree.

group_across_severities.description:
If true, we set the the group_across_severities flag on notification policies to
true, so that warn and critical notifications are grouped together.

TODO:  i don't think we export this on notification policies in the public API,
       we may need to figure out a better solution here (do we need to expose
       in both, or do we need to update the comment to stop referring to the
       option on the policy?)

exists_op_supported.description:
If true, alert rule expressions that are not in the form of "expr op number" (e.g. "sum(stuff) > 5")
will be executed as-is.
Without this set, expressions will be converted to binary expressions (e.g. "x > y" as "(x - y) > 0").

TODO: before configv1 - is this still required? does chronoctl set it?

SyncPrometheusContainerType.title: TODO: confirm naming with InfoModel team

SyntheticTestBodyAssertion.description: Asserts that the raw response body matches the target string.

SyntheticTestBodyHashAssertion.description: Asserts that a hash of the response body equals the target.

SyntheticTestBodyJSONPathAssertion.description:
Asserts that a value extracted via JSONPath matches the target.
match_type controls how multiple matches are evaluated.

SyntheticTestBodyJSONSchemaAssertion.description: Asserts that the JSON response body conforms to a JSON schema.

SyntheticTestBodyXPathAssertion.description: Asserts that a value extracted via XPath 1.0 matches the target.

SyntheticTestCertificateAssertion.description: Asserts on the leaf certificate's expiration window, in days.

SyntheticTestCertificatePropertyAssertio.description:
Asserts on a named property of the leaf certificate, e.g. subject, issuer,
or a Subject Alternative Name.

SyntheticTestConnectionAssertion.description:
Asserts on the outcome of the TCP connection attempt. Only the EQUALS
operator is supported (e.g. connection is Established).

SyntheticTestDnsRecordAssertion.description:
Asserts on DNS records returned for the query. match_scope selects whether
the operator must hold for every record of record_type or for at least
one. SOA and SRV record types are only valid with AT_LEAST_ONE.

dns_server.description: Optional explicit nameserver to query; runner default resolver if unset.

dns_server_port.description: Nameserver port; required when dns_server is set, rejected without it.

SyntheticTestDnsTestConfigAssertion.description:
Wraps the assertion types valid for DNS tests. Exactly one assertion
field must be set per entry; enforced by validation.

SyntheticTestHeaderAssertion.description: Asserts that the named response header satisfies the operator.

max_redirects.description: optional — 0-10; defaults to 10 when follow_redirects is set

allow_insecure_tls.description: Skip TLS certificate verification for HTTPS requests.

max_response_body_bytes.description: Cap on captured response-body size in bytes; 0 means use the default.

do_not_save_response_body_on_failure.description: When set, the captured body is not persisted on failed executions.

SyntheticTestHttpTestConfigAssertion.description:
Wraps the assertion types valid for HTTP tests. Exactly one assertion
field must be set per entry; enforced by validation. Each test type
declares its own Assertion wrapper enumerating the subset of
SyntheticTest-level assertion messages valid for it.

SyntheticTestMonitorConfig.description:
Alerting configuration for the test. An alert fires when at least
min_failing_locations locations are simultaneously failing within the
failing-duration window, and resolves automatically (no separate resolve
duration).

min_failing_locations.description:
Peak count of simultaneously-failing locations within the window
required to fire. Must be between 1 and the number of test locations.

failing_duration_secs.description:
Failure-duration window in seconds. 0 means alert on first failure.
Otherwise must be at least interval_secs.

annotations.description: Annotations attached to the generated alert.

notification_policy_slug.description: Notification policy that routes the generated alert.

SyntheticTestNetworkHopsAssertion.description: Asserts on the number of network hops to the target.

SyntheticTestResponseTimeAssertion.description:
Asserts that the request response time is below the target, in
milliseconds. Scope selects whether DNS resolution time is included.

SyntheticTestStatusCodeAssertion.description:
Asserts that the HTTP response status code matches the target.
`target` is a string so regex patterns (e.g. "2..") can share the field
with literal codes (e.g. "200").

SyntheticTestTcpTestConfigAssertion.description:
Wraps the assertion types valid for TCP tests. Exactly one assertion
field must be set per entry; enforced by validation.

server_name.description: SNI server name; defaults to host when unset.

SyntheticTestTlsTestConfigAssertion.description:
Wraps the assertion types valid for TLS tests. Exactly one assertion
field must be set per entry; enforced by validation.

SyntheticTestTlsVersionAssertion.description:
Asserts on the negotiated TLS version. bound selects whether the assertion
constrains the maximum or minimum acceptable version. target is a version
string, e.g. "1.2".

default_sampling_rate.description: Defines the service-wide sampling probability (in the range [0, 1]) when specific operations are not matched.

default_lower_bound_traces_per_second.description: Defines a minimum number of traces to send for ANY operation in the service, regardless of matching per operation strategy.

default_upper_bound_traces_per_second.description: Defines a maximum number of traces to send for ANY operation in the service, regardless of matching per operation strategy.

per_operation_strategies.description: Defines explicit operations-specific strategies that take precedence over the default sampling rate.

sampling_rate.description: Value in the range [0, 1] that defines the probability of sampling any trace.

max_traces_per_second.description: Maximum number of traces to sample per second.

value.description: The value of the filter compared to the target trace or span field.

min.description: Minimum number of spans that must match a span query, inclusive.

max.description: Maximum number of spans that must match a span query, inclusive.

min_secs.description: Minimum duration, in seconds, required for a span or trace to match.

max_secs.description: Maximum duration, in seconds, required for a span or trace to match.

value.description: The filter value used in comparison against match candidates.

span_scopes.description:
Specifies the span filters that define which spans contribute to the metrics calculation.
Only spans matching these filters will be included in the metrics aggregation.

tags.description: Matches the tags of the candidate span.

value.description: The value of the filter compared to the target trace or span field.

in_values.description: Values the filter tests against when using `IN` or `NOT_IN` match type.

key.description: The key or name of the span tag that this filter inspects.

tag_name.description: Tag name that uniquely identifies a tag.

description.description: Human readable description of the tag.

code.description: An optional private error code whose values are undefined.

message.description: An error message describing what went wrong.

cloudintegrationconfigGcpConfig.description: GcpConfig contains configuration for scraping GCP Cloud Monitoring metrics.

metric_groups.description: Metric groups to collect. Each group specifies a project and metric prefixes.

configunstableBasicAuth.description:
BasicAuth carries HTTP basic auth credentials used by a SyntheticTest.
Top-level so future non-HTTP test types can reuse it.

password.description:
May contain a {{VAR_NAME}} reference resolved at test execution to a
SyntheticGlobalVariable value.

configunstableCloudIntegration.description:
CloudIntegration represents a configuration for collecting metrics from a
cloud provider. Each integration contains provider-specific configuration.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the CloudIntegration was created. Cannot be set by clients.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the CloudIntegration was last updated. Cannot be set by clients.

metric_labels.description: Labels applied to all metrics emitted from this integration.

external_connection_slug.description:
Slug of the ExternalConnection used for credentials. Optional — providers
that use workload identity (e.g. GCP) leave this empty.

configunstableCloudIntegrationState.description:
State represents the operational state of a CloudIntegration.

 - STATE_ENABLED: Integration is fully enabled and actively collecting metrics.
 - STATE_DISABLED: Integration is disabled and not collecting metrics.
 - STATE_PREVIEW: Integration is in preview mode for estimating cardinality/license impact
before full enablement.

configunstableCloudIntegrationType.description:
CloudIntegrationType identifies the cloud provider for a CloudIntegration.
This is server-derived from whichever provider config is set.

 - GCP: Google Cloud Platform.

configunstableCommandCenterGroup.description: CommandCenterGroup is a group of signals tracked in the command center.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the CommandCenterGroup was created. Cannot be set by clients.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the CommandCenterGroup was last updated. Cannot be set by clients.

dry_run.description: If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating the CloudIntegration. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the CloudIntegration. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating the CommandCenterGroup. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the CommandCenterGroup. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating the LinkTemplate. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the LinkTemplate. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating the LogPrimaryKey. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the LogPrimaryKey. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating the Service. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the Service. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating the SyntheticGlobalVariable. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the SyntheticGlobalVariable. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating the SyntheticTest. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the SyntheticTest. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating the TraceJaegerRemoteSamplingStrategy. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the TraceJaegerRemoteSamplingStrategy. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating the TraceTailSamplingRules. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the TraceTailSamplingRules. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating the TraceTopTagConfig. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the TraceTopTagConfig. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

slug.description: The unique identifier of the Dashboard. If a `slug` isn't provided, one is generated based on the `name` field. You can't modify this field after the Dashboard is created.

name.description: The name of the Dashboard. You can modify this value after the Dashboard is created.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the Dashboard was created. Cannot be set by clients.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the Dashboard was last updated. Cannot be set by clients.

collection_slug.description: Required slug of the collection the dashboard belongs to.

dashboard_json.description: Required raw JSON of the dashboard.

enabled.description: Whether to override the default sample rate

sample_rate.description: A fraction of traces to keep, which should be a number between 0 and 1, inclusive

referencing_tests.description: Tests that referenced this variable at delete time.

name.description: Unique name of the LinkTemplate. Used as the link text.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the LinkTemplate was created. Cannot be set by clients.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the LinkTemplate was last updated. Cannot be set by clients.

ui_component.description: Link template is scoped to the specified string representing a UI component here.

targeted_field.description: Targeted field defines a field in a payload that should be materialized.

column_name.description: Name of the clickhouse column that this field maps to. Its an actual value that is used for the queries.

materialize_expression.description:
Materialize expression allows providing more complex instructions
how to get field value, for example using conditions, instead
of simply targeting field defined by targeted_field.
If materialize_expression is provided targeted_field should be empty.

slug.description: The unique identifier of the LogPrimaryKey. If a `slug` isn't provided, one is generated based on the `name` field. You can't modify this field after the LogPrimaryKey is created.

name.description: The name of the LogPrimaryKey. You can modify this value after the LogPrimaryKey is created.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the LogPrimaryKey was created. Cannot be set by clients.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the LogPrimaryKey was last updated. Cannot be set by clients.

slug.description: The unique identifier of the NoopEntity. If a `slug` isn't provided, one is generated based on the `name` field. You can't modify this field after the NoopEntity is created.

name.description: The name of the NoopEntity. You can modify this value after the NoopEntity is created.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the NoopEntity was created. Cannot be set by clients.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the NoopEntity was last updated. Cannot be set by clients.

slug.description: The unique identifier of the Notebook. If a `slug` isn't provided, one is generated based on the `name` field. You can't modify this field after the Notebook is created.

name.description: The name of the Notebook. You can modify this value after the Notebook is created.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the Notebook was created. Cannot be set by clients.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the Notebook was last updated. Cannot be set by clients.

collection_slug.description: Required slug of the collection the notebook belongs to.

notebook_json.description: Required raw JSON of the notebook.

slug.description: The unique identifier of the ObjectDiscoveryRule. If a `slug` isn't provided, one is generated based on the `name` field. You can't modify this field after the ObjectDiscoveryRule is created.

name.description: The name of the ObjectDiscoveryRule. You can modify this value after the ObjectDiscoveryRule is created.
The name of the SavedTraceSearch. You can modify this value after the SavedTraceSearch is created.

slug.description: The unique identifier of the SavedTraceSearch. If a `slug` isn't provided, one is generated based on the `name` field. You can't modify this field after the SavedTraceSearch is created.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the SavedTraceSearch was created. Cannot be set by clients.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the SavedTraceSearch was last updated. Cannot be set by clients.

slug.description: The unique identifier of the Service. If a `slug` isn't provided, one is generated based on the `name` field. You can't modify this field after the Service is created.

name.description: The name of the Service. You can modify this value after the Service is created.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the Service was created. Cannot be set by clients.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the Service was last updated. Cannot be set by clients.

derived_name.description: Required telemetry name of the service.

team_slug.description: Required slug of the team the service collection belongs to.

description.description: Optional description of the service collection.

notification_policy_slug.description:
Slug of the notification policy used by default for monitors in this service collection.
This is optional if the collection does not contain monitors or all of its monitors explicitly reference a policy.
This does not override the policy used when a monitor explicitly references a policy.

configunstableSloReference.description: SloReference references an SLO by slug.

configunstableSyntheticGlobalVariable.description:
SyntheticGlobalVariable is a tenant-scoped named string variable that
synthetic tests would reference inline via {{NAME}} template syntax. The
feature is deferred — see src/synthetics/docs/decision-log.md DECISION-020
for the current scope. The proto and CRUD remain in-tree but are not
referenced by any active test-side code path.

slug.description: The unique identifier of the SyntheticGlobalVariable. If a `slug` isn't provided, one is generated based on the `name` field. You can't modify this field after the SyntheticGlobalVariable is created.

name.description: The name of the SyntheticGlobalVariable. You can modify this value after the SyntheticGlobalVariable is created.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the SyntheticGlobalVariable was created. Cannot be set by clients.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the SyntheticGlobalVariable was last updated. Cannot be set by clients.

protected.description:
Always true in Phase 1; field exists so Phase 2 can introduce non-protected
variables without a schema migration.

value.description:
The variable value. Required on create. For protected variables the
server cannot read the value back, so reads always return "".

name.description: The name of the SyntheticTest. You can modify this value after the SyntheticTest is created.

slug.description: The unique identifier of the SyntheticTest. If a `slug` isn't provided, one is generated based on the `name` field. You can't modify this field after the SyntheticTest is created.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the SyntheticTest was created. Cannot be set by clients.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the SyntheticTest was last updated. Cannot be set by clients.

collection_slug.description:
Slug of the collection that owns the test. Required if `collection` isn't
set.

slug.description: The unique identifier of the TraceJaegerRemoteSamplingStrategy. If a `slug` isn't provided, one is generated based on the `name` field. You can't modify this field after the TraceJaegerRemoteSamplingStrategy is created.

name.description: The name of the TraceJaegerRemoteSamplingStrategy. You can modify this value after the TraceJaegerRemoteSamplingStrategy is created.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the TraceJaegerRemoteSamplingStrategy was created. Cannot be set by clients.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the TraceJaegerRemoteSamplingStrategy was last updated. Cannot be set by clients.

service_name.description: The name of the service this sampling strategy applies to. This must match the slug and name fields.

configunstableTraceTailSamplingRule.description: Contains configuration for one tail sampling rule.

sample_rate.description: A fraction of traces to keep, which should be a number between 0 and 1, inclusive

name.description: A human-readable name of the rule, which summarizes what it's for

system_name.description: Value used as the metric label value for metrics emitted relating to this rule.

created_at.description: When the rule was created (novel system_name)

updated_at.description: When the rule was updated (existing system_name)

configunstableTraceTailSamplingRules.description: Root object containing all tail sampling rules (for a tenant).

rules.description:
Optional. A list of rules, evaluated in order until a match is found,
and the sample rate applied, or else the default sample rate is applied.

configunstableTraceTopTagConfig.description:
TraceTopTagConfig is a singleton configuration object that specifies the
configuration for top tracing tags.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the TraceTopTagConfig was created. Cannot be set by clients.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the TraceTopTagConfig was last updated. Cannot be set by clients.

top_tags.description: The list of "top" tags. Must be unique by tag_name.

create_if_missing.description: If `true`, the TraceTailSamplingRules will be created if it does not already exist. If `false`, an error will be returned if the TraceTailSamplingRules does not already exist.

dry_run.description: If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating or updating the TraceTailSamplingRules. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the TraceTailSamplingRules. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

create_if_missing.description: If `true`, the TraceTopTagConfig will be created if it does not already exist. If `false`, an error will be returned if the TraceTopTagConfig does not already exist.

dry_run.description: If `true`, validates the specified configuration without creating or updating the TraceTopTagConfig. If the specified configuration is valid, the endpoint returns a partial response without the TraceTopTagConfig. If the specified configuration is invalid, the endpoint returns an error.

max_size.description:
Sets the preferred number of items to return per page. If set to `0`, the
server will use its default value. Regardless of the value specified, clients
must never assume how many items will be returned.

token.description:
An opaque page token that identifies which page the client should request.
An empty value indicates the first page.

next_token.description:
An opaque page token that identifies the next page of items that the
client should request. An empty value indicates that there are no
more items to return.

span.description:
Specifies the span conditions to match on. All conditions must be true in a
single span for the span to be considered a match. If `span_count` is specified,
the number of spans within the trace that match span conditions must be within
`[min, max]`. You can specify multiple span conditions, and each can be
satisfied by any number of spans within the trace.

info.description: WARNING: This API is unstable and is meant for experimentation only. Do not use this API in production. The endpoints and schemas referenced in this documentation will be changed or removed without notice and without any backwards compatibility guarantee. Note that even though this API is unstable, it is still connected to production data, so use with caution.
info.title: Config Unstable API

parameters.description:
Sets the preferred number of items to return per page. If set to `0`, the
server will use its default value. Regardless of the value specified, clients
must never assume how many items will be returned.
parameters.name: page.max_size

parameters.description:
An opaque page token that identifies which page the client should request.
An empty value indicates the first page.
parameters.name: page.token

parameters.description: Filters results by slug, where any CloudIntegration with a matching slug in the given list (and matches all other filters) will be returned.
parameters.name: slugs

parameters.description: Filters results by name, where any CloudIntegration with a matching name in the given list (and matches all other filters) will be returned.
parameters.name: names

parameters.description:
Filter by provider type.

 - GCP: Google Cloud Platform.
parameters.name: provider_types

parameters.description:
Filter by integration state.

 - STATE_ENABLED: Integration is fully enabled and actively collecting metrics.
 - STATE_DISABLED: Integration is disabled and not collecting metrics.
 - STATE_PREVIEW: Integration is in preview mode for estimating cardinality/license impact
before full enablement.
parameters.name: states

200.description: A successful response containing the created CloudIntegration.

400.description: Cannot create the CloudIntegration because the request is invalid.

409.description: Cannot create the CloudIntegration because there is a conflict with an existing CloudIntegration.

400.description: Cannot delete the CloudIntegration because it is in use.

404.description: Cannot delete the CloudIntegration because the slug does not exist.
Cannot read the CloudIntegration because the slug does not exist.

200.description: A successful response containing the updated CloudIntegration.

400.description: Cannot update the CloudIntegration because the request is invalid.

404.description: Cannot update the CloudIntegration because the slug does not exist.

409.description: Cannot update the CloudIntegration because there is a conflict with an existing CloudIntegration.

parameters.description:
Sets the preferred number of items to return per page. If set to `0`, the
server will use its default value. Regardless of the value specified, clients
must never assume how many items will be returned.
parameters.name: page.max_size

parameters.description:
An opaque page token that identifies which page the client should request.
An empty value indicates the first page.
parameters.name: page.token

parameters.description: Filters results by slug, where any CommandCenterGroup with a matching slug in the given list (and matches all other filters) will be returned.
parameters.name: slugs

parameters.description: Filters results by name, where any CommandCenterGroup with a matching name in the given list (and matches all other filters) will be returned.
parameters.name: names

200.description: A successful response containing the created CommandCenterGroup.

400.description: Cannot create the CommandCenterGroup because the request is invalid.

409.description: Cannot create the CommandCenterGroup because there is a conflict with an existing CommandCenterGroup.

400.description: Cannot delete the CommandCenterGroup because it is in use.

404.description: Cannot delete the CommandCenterGroup because the slug does not exist.
